human_verification.notes: Verified issue #1006 scope, NAICS 561 and 5614 industry specificity, intake-access-and-initiation workflow coverage, source-class coverage, scenario-to-ability mapping, refusal triggers, and specification-based runtime boundary.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit intake, prerequisite, eligibility, authorization, access, routing, handoff, queue ownership, and refusal behavior.
Required abilities across shared, intake-access-and-initiation, and cross-industry profiles are fully covered and mapped.

Scenarios include missing prerequisites, invalid or ineligible request, wrong-lane routing, access bypass refusal, contact-center routing, ambiguous ownership, incomplete handoff, low-confidence missing tenant facts, conflicting policies, tenant adaptation, and industry overgeneralization refusal.

specialist_owned_evidence: NAICS 561 administrative support and 5614 business support service context materially shape scope, artifacts, terminology, systems, and refusal behavior.
Intake is accepted only when client/SOW scope, service catalog item, requester identity, authorization, access, required documents, queue owner, and source hierarchy are explicit and satisfied.
Routing decisions preserve handoff context, current owner accountability, duplicate checks, SLA or blocker state, and evidence references.
Contact-center, document-preparation, mailroom, shared-services, and BPO intake does not become authority over the represented client service or downstream approval decision.
Runtime remains specification-based, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains after converting tenant variation to explicit retrieval, refusal, and handoff requirements.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment with clear promotion criteria to retained-implementation execution.
